prompt: **Open-ended questions in the stepper.** Some clarifying questions are genuinely open-ended, with no sensible options. Today the stepper's free-text box is a single-line `<input>`, while the wall-of-text fallback is a 3-row `<textarea>`. So always-structured would *downgrade* the answer box for open-ended asks. What should happen?
options: Always structured; also widen the stepper's free-text box to a textarea, so an open-ended ask keeps a roomy multi-line answer box — RECOMMENDED
Always structured; keep the single-line input as-is and accept the downgrade for open-ended asks
Structured only when a question has real options; open-ended asks keep the plain-string fallback

prompt: **Where should the end-to-end test start?** Nothing today covers the seam from a brainstorm-shaped `--questions @file` all the way to a rendered stepper.
options: One shared fixture JSON, pinned from both sides — RECOMMENDED
Full stack: a Playwright test that shells out to the real bin/relay
